page.title: sickle cell disease — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for sickle cell disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for sickle cell disease: 168 alterations and 508 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Antioxidant vitamin levels are depleted in sickle cell disorders, indicating that supplementation may be of value.
Broccoli sprout homogenate is safe in stable SCD patients and may induce changes in gene expression levels.
Chronic, oral, low-dose vitamin C supplementation equilibrates autonomic function following change in posture with those of non-sickle cell anemia subjects..
Citrulline and spirulina may have a therapeutic role in the management of sickle cell disease.
Cost-effectiveness analysis of adding omega-3 or vitamin D supplementation to standard therapy in treating painful crises of pediatric sickle cell disease patients.
Guided imagery is an effective technique for managing and limiting sickle cell disease-related pain in a pediatric population.
he effect of warmth or/and vitamin E supplementation has a positive effect on forearm blood flow and forearm vascular resistance in sickle cell and non sickle cell anaemia subjects.
Inadequate nutritional intake, weight and BMI have a significant impact on sickle cell disease severity indices.
Increases in plasma vitamin E levels improve vascular responses and lipid peroxidation in sickle cell anaemia subjects.
Influence of lime juice on the severity of sickle cell anemia.
Magnesium supplements reduce erythrocyte dehydration in patients with sickle cell disease.
Oral glutamine supplementation decreases resting energy expenditure in children and adolescents with sickle cell anemia.
Prepubertal children with SCD-SS may have zinc deficiency and may benefit from zinc supplementation to improve linear growth and weight gain.
Rehmannia contains a compound which may be of value in treating sickle cell anemia.
Risk factors for vitamin D deficiency in sickle cell disease.
The fixed oil extracted from Nigella sativa seeds has an in vitro antisickling activity.
Vitamin C and/or warmth have a positive effect on forearm blood flow and vascular resistance in sickle cell anaemia subjects.
Vitamin C supplementation may have value in sickle cell anemia.
Zinc and antioxidant vitamin deficiency in patients with severe sickle cell anemia.
High dose vitamin D may have a therapeutic role in certain cases of chronic pain associated with sickle cell anemia.
Investigating the potential use of andrographolide as a coadjuvant in sickle cell anemia therapy.
Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in a previously vaccinated adolescent female with Sickle cell disease.
N-acetylcysteine in the management of acute liver failure from sickle cell hepatic crisis.
Acupuncture may be a beneficial adjuvant approach to the current limited pain management therapies in sickle cell disease.
Curcumin and CoQ10 independently as well as in combination ameliorate chronic hyperalgesia in sickle mice.
Curcumin may represent a new horizon in managing iron overload-induced toxicity as well as in pathological diseases characterized by hepatic iron accumulation.
Dietary supplementation with sulforaphane attenuates liver damage and heme overload in a sickle cell disease murine model.
Neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic activity of palmitoylethanolamide in sickle cell mice.
"Sickle cell anemia: a potential nutritional approach for a molecular disease."
Aged garlic extract and other antioxidant nutritional supplements exhibit potentially therpeutic properties in sickle erythrocytes.
Antioxidant-rich nutraceutical as a therapeutic strategy for sickle cell disease.
Suma appears to have valuable application in the treatment of sickle cells.
